channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: AI RF Satellites Could Transform Indian Navy’s Ability to Track “Dark Ships” and Stealth Naval Movements
item.description: India’s maritime domain is entering an era where traditional surveillance tools are increasingly insufficient against adversaries employing deception, emission control (EMCON), and transponder shutdown tactics. In this context, Xovian Aerospace’s development of an AI-powered radio frequency (RF) satellite constellation represents a potentially transformative capability for the Indian Navy, particularly in tracking “dark ships” and monitoring contested sea lanes in real time.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Lockheed Martin Signals Customized C-130J Offer for IAF’s 60-Aircraft MTA Requirement, Highlights “Make in India” Production Pitch
item.description: Lockheed Martin has indicated that it is prepared to offer a tailored version of the C-130J Super Hercules for the Indian Air Force’s Medium Transport Aircraft (MTA) requirement, even as it refrained from disclosing specific configuration details due to the competitive nature of the programme. The response suggests that the company is positioning a customized solution aligned with India’s operational and environmental demands, while simultaneously leveraging its deep industrial footprint in the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: How Iran’s Low-Cost Hormuz Playbook Gave New Delhi the Blueprint to Dominate China in the Indian Ocean – 100 Times Stronger
item.description: Iran has shown the world how a resource-constrained power can turn a narrow chokepoint into a strategic weapon. By deploying cheap sea mines, kamikaze drone boats, anti-ship missiles, and electronic warfare from its rugged coastline, Tehran has repeatedly threatened to shut down the Strait of Hormuz – the artery carrying 20 per cent of global oil. It does not need to sink every tanker or win a conventional battle. A few sporadic hits, floating mines, and jammed navigation systems are enough to spike insurance premiums, scare shipping companies away, and paralyze energy markets. This asymmetric playbook – low-cost, high-impact, geography-driven – has become the textbook for weaker navies facing superior fleets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army vs Pakistani Army Drone Race: How FPV, Attack Drones and Indigenous Innovation Are Leaving Pakistan Far Behind
item.description: The drone battlefield along the India-Pakistan border has transformed from a niche capability into the decisive edge of modern warfare. As both armies race to integrate First Person View (FPV) drones, kamikaze strike systems, loitering munitions and swarm technologies, the Indian Army has pulled decisively ahead. Through a powerful combination of in-house defence research and explosive private sector participation, India is not just matching Pakistan’s drone fleet but redefining the scale, speed and self-reliance of unmanned operations in one of the world’s most volatile regions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BEL to build new factory UP Defence Corridor for QRSAM, Kusha Air Defence System and Next-Gen Radar Manufacturing Hub
item.description: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) has taken a major step in expanding India’s indigenous defence manufacturing ecosystem by acquiring 75 hectares of land in the Uttar Pradesh Defence Industrial Corridor at Chitrakoot. The land allotment was formally handed over by Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath to BEL Chairman and Managing Director Manoj Jain, marking a significant milestone in the state’s efforts to position itself as a key hub for defence production.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GRSE Targets AI-Driven Solution to Minimise Deformation in High-Strength Steel Warships
item.description: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE) has launched a new DRISHTI challenge aimed at addressing a critical manufacturing issue in modern warship construction—deformation in thin plate high-strength steel structures. The initiative seeks the development of an advanced software-based system capable of optimising welding and fit-up processes to ensure structural integrity and production efficiency.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iranians Unleash Scathing Mockery on Pakistan on X: Kashmir Declared Indian Territory, 1971 Defeat Revived, and Mediation Efforts Ridiculed
item.description: In a surprising online backlash, Iranian users on X (formerly Twitter) have launched a wave of sharp, often vulgar criticism against Pakistan, turning diplomatic friction into a public spectacle. From declaring Jammu and Kashmir as eternal Indian land to reminding Pakistan of its humiliating 1971 defeat and dismissing its mediation attempts in the US-Iran conflict, Persian-language posts and translated threads are flooding timelines with anti-Pakistan sentiment. The trigger appears to be Pakistan’s high-profile but faltering bid to mediate between Iran and the United States amid escalating West Asia tensions in April 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Foreign Secretary Misri reviews defence, trade ties with senior US officials
item.description: India’s Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri met senior US defence official Michael Duffey and discussed ways to further deepen the defence industrial, technology and supply chain linkages between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath Singh congratulates Phan Van Giang on appointment as Vietnam’s Defence Minister
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Thursday extended warm greetings to General Phan Van Giang on his appointment as the Deputy Prime Minister and Defence Minister of Vietnam, stating that India looks forward to deepening longstanding defence ties.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Worried by rising oil prices, US pushed Pakistan to broker truce with Iran: report
item.description: Worried about surging oil prices and surprised by a resilient Iranian regime, the White House pushed Pakistan to broker a temporary ceasefire with Tehran, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: West Asia War: Rajnath Singh leads IGoM in bolstering India’s readiness ?
item.description: The Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM), chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, held its third meeting to assess India’s readiness in the face of the evolving situation in West Asia. ?
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DG Shipping Asks Ports To Provide Concessions To Exporters, Advises Seafarers To Remain Safe
item.description: Amid the ongoing West Asia crisis, Directorate General of Shipping (DG Shipping) has directed ports to ensure concessions for exporters with stranded cargo in the war-affected Persian Gulf region.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Surrendered Naxalites to boost anti-IED skills of security forces in Chhattisgarh
item.description: The Chhattisgarh police have roped in surrendered Naxalites with expertise in improvised explosive devices (IEDs) to train security personnel, aiming to enhance their operational capabilities, officials said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAEA Director General praises India’s major nuclear milestone at Kalpakkam
item.description: Rafael Mariano Grossi, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General, has warmly appreciated India’s achievement in attaining criticality of the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 14.5 kg of IED recovered & neutralised in J&K’s Shopian, averts major terror incident
item.description: The Indian Army’s Chinar Corps in a joint operation along with the Jammu and Kashmir Police averted a major terror incident by recovering and neutralising 14.5 kg of Improvised Explosive Device (IED) near Chitragam in Zainapora area of Jammu and Kashmir’s Shopian district, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
